name: Kerson Leong, violin
description:
Winner of the prestigious International Menuhin Violin Competition, Kerson Leong has been described as "not just one of Canada's greatest violinists but one of the greatest violinists, period". 

He has performed with Brussels, Oslo, Royal Philharmonic and Istanbul, Singapore, Toronto, Wuppertal and Montréal Symphony Orchestras. As a sought-after soloist, Kerson has appeared at Carnegie and Wigmore Halls, Auditorium du Louvre, and National Center for the Performing Arts, Beijing. 

He plays the 'ex Bohrer, Baumgartner' Guarneri del Gesu violin courtesy of Canimex Inc. Drummondville.

His virtuosic recital includes violin works by Ysaÿe (Sonata No. 3 "Ballade" & Sonata No. 4 in E minor), Kreisler's Recitativo and Scherzo, and Bach's Chaconne from Partita No. 2.

name: Community Environment Day at Woodside Square
description:
Join Councillor Myers (Scarborough North) and the City of Toronto to help reduce the amount of waste going to landfill. Drop off household items for donation, electronics for reuse or recycling and household hazardous waste for proper disposal. 

Pick up free bagged compost while supplies last and kitchen catchers for organics ($7 each – cash only).

name: Yonge Perspective II
description:
See Downtown Yonge through a different lens!
⁠
As part of the CONTACT Photography Festival, Yonge Perspective II returns to College Park, featuring a curated outdoor photography exhibit.
⁠
Take a moment to wander through the Yonge Street entrance and discover unique perspectives of our neighbourhood captured by local photographers.⁠
⁠
Stay tuned and come explore with us!
